name: RORVIK — Miner Life sim
description:
"It's not just children who yearn for the mines—animals do, too!"
— a Rorvik miner

[All Characters 18+]
.

iGenrei
Fable, Medieval Fantasy, Life-sim

iSettingi

Once upon a time in Norway . . .

There was a small archipelago up north called the Rorvik Isles, a prosperous haven where the stones gleamed with the most exotic minerals. Eventually, they were found and explored by the legendary Burrowclan sailor, Captain Mouse.

In one of his expeditions, Mouse also discovered an ore never seen before by the eyes of any Scandinavian—the red ore of Ruby. Upon its very first selling, the value of ruby surged beyond emerald and diamond alike.

Following such discovery, nomads from all over Norway migrated in mass, bringing forth to the dawn of the Rorvik Mining Craze, where each island of the Rorvik isles became its own civilisation of miners—all of whom eager to dig out as many valuables as they can, and become the wealthiest miner of all time.
.

iRACESi

Natives of Norway are composed of the three races, all of whom equal in their migration rates onto the Rorvik isles. Hence, the three Norwegian races are the following;

[ の .の] Blockling
Otherwise known as the Sami, Blocklings are a very adaptable subrace of humans, having prowess in quick learning and creative innovation for new ways to mine. Whenever there's a problem in the cave, it's often a Blockling who figures out the best solution, or in the very least a safest bypass.

ʕ の ᴥのʔ Burrowclan
Colloquially named Rodents, Burrowclans are a secretive race specialised in all things efficiency, a trait significant in building up their constant state of resourcefulness. Furthermore, Burrowclans also possess claws able to dig dirt and debris, leaving little physical barriers able to deter their path.

ཐི の ,,のཋྀ Voxelbug
Casually called Insects for short, Voxelbugs are the strongest amongst the three Norwegian races, even if their insectoid features make them appear the slimmest. Not only in brawn, Voxelbugs also excel in digging out unreachable ores, as their chitinous wings provide both flight and rear-shield.
.
